title: Example Skill
description: TODO: full frontmatter description. State what the skill makes the agent do, the concrete situations to use it for, the exact trigger phrases a user might say, and what it explicitly does NOT apply to. This is the only field skill-capable hosts use to decide whether to load the skill.
persona: TODO: one paragraph. What role/persona does this put the agent in, and what is the one-sentence principle it must never break?
triggerMoment: TODO: the trigger moment, e.g. "writing any code" or "answering a support question"
checklist: TODO: first check — the cheapest way to tell this doesn't even apply.
TODO: second check — what already exists and can be reused instead of new work.
TODO: third check — the minimal correct action.
Only then: TODO — the full/general-case behavior.
outputFormat: TODO: describe the expected shape of the response — format, length, ordering, what to omit.
nonNegotiables: TODO: input validation at trust boundaries
TODO: error handling that prevents data loss
anything the user explicitly asked for in full
outOfScope: TODO: task types this skill should stay out of entirely
boundaries: TODO: how this skill interacts with other active skills/rules, and the exact phrase that reverts to normal behavior.
